annotations.label: Monoid

anchor.snippet: is a monoid if it satisfies the following two axioms

annotations.note: `Monoid` extends `Semigroup` and `MulOneClass`, encoding associativity and a two-sided identity exactly as in the article.

annotations.label: Group as special monoid

anchor.snippet: A group is a special case of a monoid where every element has an inverse

annotations.note: `Group` extends `DivInvMonoid` (hence `Monoid`) adding `inv_mul_cancel`, so it is a monoid in which every element is invertible.

annotations.label: Submonoid

anchor.snippet: A submonoid of a monoid

annotations.note: `Submonoid` bundles a subset closed under multiplication and containing 1, matching the article's definition.

annotations.label: Closed subset need not be a submonoid

anchor.snippet: is not always a submonoid

annotations.note: Mathlib distinguishes `Subsemigroup` (closed under multiplication) from `Submonoid` (also containing 1), but the specific claim that a closed subset can fail to be a submonoid is not stated as a theorem.

annotations.label: Generating set / finitely generated monoid

anchor.snippet: is said to generate

annotations.note: `Submonoid.closure` gives the submonoid generated by a set and `Monoid.FG` is the finitely-generated typeclass (top submonoid is finitely generated).

annotations.label: Commutative monoid

anchor.snippet: is called a commutative monoid

annotations.note: `CommMonoid` extends `Monoid` with `CommSemigroup`, i.e. a monoid with commutative operation.

annotations.label: Algebraic preordering

anchor.snippet: endowed with its algebraic preordering

annotations.note: The relation a ≤ b ⇔ ∃c, b = a*c is the divisibility preorder (`Dvd`, `dvd_refl`/`dvd_trans`) but is not registered as a named algebraic-preorder structure.

annotations.label: Order-unit

anchor.snippet: An order-unit of a commutative monoid

annotations.note: No notion of order-unit of a commutative monoid (an element dominating all others up to scaling) is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Trace monoid

anchor.snippet: is a trace monoid

annotations.note: Trace (partially commutative) monoids are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Boolean operator monoids

anchor.snippet: These four each make the set {False, True} a commutative monoid

annotations.note: `Bool` carries a `BooleanAlgebra`/lattice structure but no built-in `CommMonoid` instances for and/or/xor in core Mathlib.

annotations.label: Natural numbers monoid

anchor.snippet: The set of natural numbers

annotations.note: ℕ has both `Nat.instCommMonoid` (multiplication) and `Nat.instAddCommMonoid` (addition).

annotations.label: Positive integers under multiplication

anchor.snippet: The set of positive integers

annotations.note: ℕ+ (`PNat`) has a `CancelCommMonoid` instance under multiplication.

annotations.label: Subsets under intersection

anchor.snippet: is a commutative monoid under intersection

annotations.note: `Set` is a (bounded) lattice with `⊓` = intersection and identity `univ`, but no `CommMonoid` instance for intersection is registered.

annotations.label: Subsets under union

anchor.snippet: is a commutative monoid under union

annotations.note: `SetSemiring α` (a type alias of `Set α`) carries an `AddCommMonoid` with `∪` as addition and `∅` as identity.

annotations.label: Bounded semilattice monoid

anchor.snippet: every bounded semilattice is an idempotent commutative monoid

annotations.note: Mathlib has `SemilatticeSup`/`SemilatticeInf` with `OrderBot`/`OrderTop`, but does not register an idempotent commutative monoid instance derived from a bounded semilattice.

annotations.label: Trivial monoid

anchor.snippet: forms the trivial (one-element) monoid

annotations.note: `PUnit` has a `CommGroup` (hence `CommMonoid`) instance, giving the one-element monoid.

annotations.label: Groups as monoids

anchor.snippet: Every group is a monoid and every abelian group a commutative monoid

annotations.note: `Group` extends `Monoid` and `CommGroup` extends `CommMonoid`, so every (abelian) group is a (commutative) monoid by instance inheritance.

annotations.label: Adjoining an identity to a semigroup

anchor.snippet: Any semigroup S may be turned into a monoid simply by adjoining an element e not in S

annotations.note: `WithOne α` adjoins a unit, with `instMonoid [Semigroup α]` making it a monoid.

annotations.label: Underlying set of a ring

anchor.snippet: The underlying set of any ring

annotations.note: `Ring` extends `Monoid` (via `MonoidWithZero`), so a ring's multiplicative structure is a monoid by instance inheritance.

annotations.label: Matrices under addition/multiplication

anchor.snippet: The set of all n by n matrices over a given ring

annotations.note: Square matrices `Matrix n n α` form a `Semiring`, providing both an additive commutative monoid and a multiplicative monoid.

annotations.label: Free monoid of strings

anchor.snippet: The set of all finite strings over some fixed alphabet

annotations.note: `FreeMonoid α := List α` is the free monoid on an alphabet (strings under concatenation).

annotations.label: Opposite monoid

anchor.snippet: the opposite monoid M op has the same carrier set and identity element as M

annotations.note: `MulOpposite α` (`αᵐᵒᵖ`) has a `Monoid` instance with reversed multiplication.

annotations.label: Direct product of monoids

anchor.snippet: called the direct product

annotations.note: `M × N` gets a `Monoid` instance with componentwise operations.

annotations.label: Functions into a monoid

anchor.snippet: The set of all functions from a given set to M is also a monoid

annotations.note: `Pi.monoid` gives the pointwise monoid structure on dependent functions into monoids.

annotations.label: Power set monoid

anchor.snippet: consider its power set

annotations.note: The power set `Set M` becomes a commutative monoid under union via `SetSemiring`'s `AddCommMonoid` instance.

annotations.label: Full transformation monoid

anchor.snippet: the full transformation monoid

annotations.note: `Function.End α := α → α` has a `Monoid` instance under composition, i.e. the full transformation monoid.

annotations.label: Endomorphism monoid

anchor.snippet: forms a monoid under composition of morphisms

annotations.note: `CategoryTheory.End X` (endomorphisms of an object) carries a `Monoid` instance under composition.

annotations.label: Monoid of compact surfaces under connected sum

anchor.snippet: The set of homeomorphism classes of compact surfaces with the connected sum

annotations.note: The connected-sum monoid of homeomorphism classes of compact surfaces is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cyclic monoid

anchor.snippet: Let ⟨ f ⟩ be a cyclic monoid of order n

annotations.note: `Submonoid.powers` is the submonoid generated by a single element, but the finite cyclic-monoid example with its index/period is not separately formalized.

annotations.label: Cyclic group from cyclic monoid (k = 0)

anchor.snippet: then the function f is a permutation

annotations.note: The specific result that a cyclic monoid with index 0 is a cyclic group is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of the identity element

anchor.snippet: the identity element e is unique

annotations.note: The identity is structurally fixed by `One`/`MulOneClass` and its uniqueness is immediate, but no standalone uniqueness-of-identity theorem is named.

annotations.label: Product of a sequence

anchor.snippet: one can define the product

annotations.note: `List.prod` defines the product of a finite sequence in a monoid (with `Multiset.prod`/`Finset.prod` for unordered/indexed variants).

annotations.label: Nonnegative integer powers

anchor.snippet: one can define nonnegative integer powers of an element

annotations.note: `Monoid.npow` (exposed via `HPow`/`^`) defines nonnegative integer powers with `npow_zero` and `npow_succ`.

annotations.label: Invertible element

anchor.snippet: An element x is called invertible if there exists an element y

annotations.note: `IsUnit a` asserts existence of a two-sided inverse (`Invertible`/`Units` provide bundled forms).

annotations.label: Uniqueness of inverses

anchor.snippet: Inverses, if they exist, are unique

annotations.note: `inv_unique : x*y = 1 → x*z = 1 → y = z` proves two-sided inverses are unique in a monoid.

annotations.label: Negative powers

anchor.snippet: one can define negative powers of x

annotations.note: `zpow` defines integer (including negative) powers for invertible elements in a `DivInvMonoid`/`Group`.

annotations.label: Invertible elements form a group

anchor.snippet: The set of all invertible elements in a monoid

annotations.note: The units `αˣ` of a monoid form a `Group` via `Units.instGroup`.

annotations.label: Not every monoid embeds in a group

anchor.snippet: Not every monoid sits inside a group

annotations.note: Mathlib does not prove the negative result that some monoids fail to embed into any group.

annotations.label: Cancellation property

anchor.snippet: has the cancellation property (or is cancellative)

annotations.note: `IsCancelMul`/`CancelMonoid` express two-sided cancellativity.

annotations.label: Grothendieck group embedding

anchor.snippet: can always be embedded in a group via the Grothendieck group construction

annotations.note: `Algebra.GrothendieckGroup.of_injective` shows the map into the Grothendieck group is injective when the commutative monoid is cancellative.

annotations.label: Finite cancellative monoid is a group

anchor.snippet: If a monoid has the cancellation property and is finite , then it is in fact a group

annotations.note: `LeftCancelMonoid.groupOfFinite` upgrades a finite (left-)cancellative monoid to a group.

annotations.label: Cancellative elements form a submonoid

anchor.snippet: The right- and left-cancellative elements of a monoid each in turn form a submonoid

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define the submonoid of left- or right-cancellative elements of a monoid.

annotations.label: Commutativity suffices for Grothendieck construction

anchor.snippet: commutativity is sufficient

annotations.note: `Algebra.GrothendieckGroup` and its universal `lift` are defined for any `CommMonoid`, requiring only commutativity (not cancellativity).

annotations.label: Inverse monoid

anchor.snippet: An inverse monoid is a monoid where for every a in M

annotations.note: Inverse monoids (unique generalized inverses) are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cancellative inverse monoid is a group

anchor.snippet: If an inverse monoid is cancellative, then it is a group

annotations.note: Since inverse monoids are not defined, this implication is not formalized.

annotations.label: Zerosumfree monoid

anchor.snippet: a zerosumfree monoid is an additively written monoid

annotations.note: The zerosumfree (conical) monoid property is not defined as such in Mathlib.

annotations.label: M-act

anchor.snippet: a (left) M -act (or left act over M )

annotations.note: `MulAction M α` (with `Monoid M`) is exactly a left act of a monoid on a set.

annotations.label: Operator monoid

anchor.snippet: is also known as an operator monoid

annotations.note: `MulAction.toEndHom : M →* Function.End α` realizes the action as operators, but the operator monoid as a named quotient/image is not separately defined.

annotations.label: Monoid homomorphism

anchor.snippet: A homomorphism between two monoids

annotations.note: `MonoidHom` (`M →* N`) preserves multiplication and the identity.

annotations.label: Semigroup homomorphism need not be a monoid homomorphism

anchor.snippet: Not every semigroup homomorphism between monoids is a monoid homomorphism

annotations.note: Mathlib separates `MulHom` (multiplication-preserving) from `MonoidHom` (also preserving 1), but the claim that the former need not be the latter is not stated as a theorem.

annotations.label: Semigroup homomorphism between groups is a group homomorphism

anchor.snippet: a semigroup homomorphism between groups is always a group homomorphism

annotations.note: `MonoidHom.mk'` builds a monoid hom into a group from only `map_mul`, proving `map_one` automatically.

annotations.label: Monoid isomorphism

anchor.snippet: A bijective monoid homomorphism is called a monoid isomorphism

annotations.note: `MulEquiv` (`M ≃* N`) is a multiplicative (monoid) isomorphism.

annotations.label: Monoid presentation

anchor.snippet: Monoids may be given a presentation

annotations.note: `PresentedMonoid rels` is the monoid with given generators and relations, as a quotient of the free monoid by the congruence generated by `rels`.

annotations.label: Symmetric closure of a relation

anchor.snippet: one defines its symmetric closure as

annotations.note: `Relation.SymmGen r a b := r a b ∨ r b a` is the symmetric closure of a relation.

annotations.label: Bicyclic monoid

anchor.snippet: is the equational presentation for the bicyclic monoid

annotations.note: While `PresentedMonoid` can express it, the bicyclic monoid is not defined as a named example in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Plactic monoid of degree 2

anchor.snippet: is the plactic monoid of degree 2

annotations.note: The plactic monoid is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Monoid as one-object category

anchor.snippet: A monoid is, essentially, the same thing as a category with a single object

annotations.note: `SingleObj M` is a one-object category whose endomorphism monoid of the single object is `M`.

annotations.label: Category construction from a monoid

anchor.snippet: one can construct a small category with only one object

annotations.note: `SingleObj M` carries a `Category` instance built from the monoid operation, realizing this construction.

annotations.label: Monoid homomorphisms as functors

anchor.snippet: monoid homomorphisms are just functors between single object categories

annotations.note: `SingleObj.mapHom : (M →* N) ≃ (SingleObj M ⥤ SingleObj N)` gives the bijection between monoid homs and functors.

annotations.label: Category of monoids Mon

anchor.snippet: also form their own category

annotations.note: `MonCat` is the category of monoids with monoid homomorphisms.

annotations.label: Monoid object

anchor.snippet: There is also a notion of monoid object

annotations.note: `MonObj X` is a monoid object in a monoidal category (bundled as `Mon C`).

annotations.label: Fold operation

anchor.snippet: the fold operation is defined as follows

annotations.note: `List.prod`/`List.foldr` provide the monoid fold over lists, but no general monoid-fold abstraction is named as such.

anchors.snippet: the fold operation is defined as follows

annotations.label: Complete monoid

anchor.snippet: A complete monoid is a commutative monoid equipped with an infinitary sum operation

annotations.note: Complete monoids (with an axiomatized infinitary sum) are not formalized; `tsum` is topological rather than this algebraic notion.

anchors.snippet: A complete monoid is a commutative monoid equipped with an infinitary sum operation

anchors.value: {\displaystyle \sum _{j\in J}{\sum _{i\in I_{j}}{m_{i}}}=\sum _{i\in I}m_{i}\quad {\text{ if }}\bigcup _{j\in J}I_{j}=I{\text{ and }}I_{j}\cap I_{j'}=\emptyset \quad {\text{ for }}j\neq j'}

annotations.label: Ordered commutative monoid

anchor.snippet: An ordered commutative monoid is a commutative monoid M together with a partial ordering

annotations.note: `IsOrderedMonoid` (with `IsOrderedAddMonoid` additively) axiomatizes a commutative monoid whose order is compatible with the operation.

annotations.label: Continuous monoid

anchor.snippet: A continuous monoid is an ordered commutative monoid

annotations.note: Continuous monoids (directed sups exist and distribute over the operation) are not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: A continuous monoid is an ordered commutative monoid

annotations.label: Continuous monoid is complete

anchor.snippet: If ( M , ≤) is a continuous monoid, then for any index set I

annotations.note: As continuous and complete monoids are undefined in Mathlib, this implication is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: If ( M , ≤) is a continuous monoid, then for any index set I
